title: Fallback Tools
concept: Recommending alternative tools or queries when a primary tool fails to return expected data.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: what does a retry strategy do when a tool hits a network timeout or rate limit?

options.text: It automatically tries again a few times before bothering the model
It asks the user to fix the internet

exercises.explain: Retries handle temporary hiccups by trying the same action again. If those retries keep failing, that's when we need a new plan, like a fallback tool.

cards.title: Plan B in Your Pocket
cards.body: Your favorite maps app is down, so you open a different one to find your route. An AI agent can do the same: if one tool fails, it switches to a backup.

cards.title: What Is a Fallback Tool?
cards.body: A fallback tool is a backup option an agent uses when its primary tool fails to return good data. Instead of giving up, the agent tries an alternative tool or a different query.

cards.title: The Closed Restaurant
cards.body: Your first-choice pizza place is closed, so you order from the second place on your list. A fallback tool is that second place: a ready backup so you still get dinner.

cards.title: Search Swap
cards.body: An agent asks a live weather API but gets nothing back. It falls back to a cached weather report or a different weather service, then answers the user anyway.

cards.title: Myth: Failing Means Stopping
cards.body: Some think one failed tool means the whole task fails. Not true! A smart agent recovers by trying a fallback tool or rewording the query before giving up.

cards.title: Give Up vs. Fall Back
cards.body: When a tool fails, the agent chooses: quit, or switch to a backup.

left.text: Tool fails, agent stops and says 'I can't help.'

right.text: Tool fails, agent tries a backup tool and finishes the task.

exercises.prompt: An agent's primary flight-price tool returns an error. What is the BEST fallback move?

options.text: Try a different flight-price tool or reword the query, then answer
Immediately tell the user the task is impossible
Keep calling the exact same broken tool forever
Make up a fake price to seem helpful

exercises.explain: A fallback tool is an alternative source. Switching to another tool or query gives the agent a real chance to get the data instead of quitting or inventing an answer.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A fallback tool is only useful after retries on the primary tool have failed.
exercises.explain: Retries handle quick, temporary glitches. If the primary tool still won't work after retrying, that's exactly when a fallback tool takes over.

exercises.sentence: When the primary tool fails, the agent switches to a fallback tool instead of giving up.
exercises.explain: A fallback tool is the backup the agent turns to when the main tool can't deliver the data.

exercises.prompt: Which response was written by an AI agent using a smart fallback?

options.text: The main map service is down, so I used a backup map provider. Your route is 12 minutes.
The map service is down. Task failed.
I couldn't reach the map, so please look it up yourself.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated answer recovers by switching to a backup provider and still delivers the result, which is exactly what fallback tools are for.

exercises.prompt: An agent needs stock data. It retries the primary API twice, but both retries fail. What is the best full recovery path?

options.text: Since retries failed, switch to a fallback data source or reworded query; if that also fails, tell the user clearly
Retry the same failing API 100 more times and never stop
Skip fallbacks and immediately invent a stock price
Crash silently and give no response at all

exercises.explain: Good recovery is layered: retry temporary failures first, then fall back to an alternative tool or query, and only report failure honestly if every option is exhausted. This keeps the agent helpful without making up data.
